diagrams.title: Key Types Hierarchy in Relational Model
diagrams.description: Shows the hierarchical relationship between different types of keys from most general to most specific
elements: Super Key (Top - most attributes, includes all)
diagrams.purpose: Helps students understand the inheritance relationship between keys and why some keys are subsets of others

diagrams.title: Relation Structure Example
diagrams.description: A visual representation of a relation with all its components labeled
elements: Tuples/Rows: (E1, John, IT, 50000), (E2, Sarah, HR, 45000), (E3, Mike, IT, 55000)
diagrams.purpose: Clarifies the fundamental terminology of relational database structure

diagrams.title: Foreign Key Relationship Between Tables
diagrams.description: Demonstrates how two tables are connected through primary and foreign keys
elements: Table 2: Employee(EmpID PK, Name, DeptID FK)
Referential Integrity Constraint: Employee.DeptID must exist in Department.DeptID or be NULL
Visual: Arrow from Employee.DeptID pointing to Department.DeptID labeled 'REFERENCES'
diagrams.purpose: Shows how referential integrity is maintained through primary-foreign key relationships
